I agree that pulse oximetry primarily functions by measuring oxygen saturation; however, its utility extends beyond just determining oxygen levels—it can indirectly indicate changes in circulation health, such as shifts due to inadequate ventilation. This broader view supports the use of pulse oximetry not only for direct oxygen measurement but also for assessing general hemodynamic stability during anesthesia induction.
